engineIntelligence.h2: Renault Safrane Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Renault Safrane in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Renault Safrane G8T engine - diesel 2.2L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbocharger wear - oil seal degradation causes boost loss and blue smoke from 90k miles.
EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup restricts flow, triggering limp mode on high-mileage units.

cards.altText: Renault Safrane Z7X engine - petrol 3.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Head gasket failure - coolant/oil mixing common on high-mileage V6 units, white smoke symptom.
Timing belt neglect - catastrophic interference failure if replacement interval missed.

cards.altText: Renault Safrane N7U engine - petrol 2.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Water pump failure - coolant leaks and overheating reported on units over 80k miles.
Ignition coil wear - misfires and rough idle common on neglected high-mileage examples.

engineGuide.h2: Renault Safrane Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Renault Safrane across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.1L Diesel
entries.history: Early turbo diesel for Safrane, robust block but limited power; sought by classic restorers.
commonFailures: Turbo oil seal wear - blue smoke and boost loss common after 90k miles on high-mileage units.
Injector pump wear - hard starting and rough idle reported on neglected examples.
entries.cta: Get quotes for S8U 700 Renault Safrane engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Diesel
entries.history: Refined successor to S8U with improved torque; the mainstream diesel choice for RN/RT/RXE.
compatibleVariants: RN 2.2 dT, RT 2.2 dT, RXE 2.2 dT, Initiale 2.2 dT
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup triggers limp mode; cleaning or replacement often required.
Turbocharger wear - oil leaks and reduced boost common on units over 100k miles.
entries.cta: Get quotes for G8T 700 Renault Safrane engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Diesel
entries.history: Late-production G8T with revised ECU mapping; fitted to top-spec Initiale models.
commonFailures: Injector calibration drift - rough running and increased fuel consumption on high-mileage units.
Cooling system leaks - water pump and hose degradation common on classic examples.
entries.cta: Get quotes for G8T 706 Renault Safrane engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Entry-level petrol engine for RN/RT; reliable SOHC design with timing belt service critical.
commonFailures: Timing belt failure - interference engine; snapped belt causes catastrophic valve damage.
Water pump leaks - coolant loss and overheating reported on units over 80k miles.
entries.cta: Get quotes for N7Q 700 Renault Safrane engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol
entries.history: Mid-range performance petrol for RXE; 12-valve design with stronger bottom end than 2.0L.
commonFailures: Ignition coil failure - misfires and rough idle common on high-mileage neglected units.
Valve stem seal wear - increased oil consumption and blue smoke on startup after 90k miles.
entries.cta: Get quotes for N7U 700 Renault Safrane engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol V6
entries.history: Iconic flagship V6 for RXE; smooth, refined power but head gasket weakness on high mileage.
commonFailures: Head gasket failure - coolant/oil mixing, white smoke; most common catastrophic failure on V6.
Timing belt neglect - interference design; missed service causes piston/valve collision.
entries.cta: Get quotes for Z7X 700 Renault Safrane engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol V6
entries.history: Revised V6 for Initiale with updated ECU and emissions; sought by classic luxury enthusiasts.
commonFailures: Oil consumption increase - piston ring wear on high-mileage units; blue smoke under acceleration.
Camshaft sensor failure - no-start or intermittent stalling reported on neglected examples.
entries.cta: Get quotes for Z7X 701 Renault Safrane engine replacement

Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: Renault Safrane Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Renault Safrane's Z7X V6 petrol and G8T diesel engines dominate UK replacement enquiries, with head gasket failure and turbo wear the most common catastrophic issues. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address these original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.group: Renault Safrane V6 Head Gasket Failure
problems.h4: Renault Safrane V6 Head Gasket Failure - Why Z7X Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Renault Safrane RXE 3.0 V6, Initiale 3.0 V6 (1993-2001, Z7X 700/Z7X 701)
problems.rootCause: Cylinder head warpage under thermal stress combined with original gasket material degradation allows coolant/oil cross-contamination.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Head Gasket Replacement - Remove cylinder head, machine surface, fit upgraded multi-layer steel gasket
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Head removal, machining, new gasket, timing belt kit, fluids
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block/head undamaged; 40-60k mile expectancy post-repair

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Z7X Engine - Complete short block rebuild with upgraded components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Bench-tested rebuilt engine, new belt kit, upgraded gaskets, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage cars; addresses root cause, 60k+ mile expectancy

problems.recommendation: On a classic Safrane RXE/Initiale worth £2,000-£6,000, a full rebuilt Z7X engine often represents better long-term value than a head gasket repair alone, especially if the car has exceeded 100k miles. We recommend comparing quotes for both options.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Z7X V6 engine prices for your Renault Safrane

problems.h4: Renault Safrane Turbocharger Failure Diesel - Why G8T Engines Lose Boost
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger oil seal degradation allows oil into intake/exhaust; bearing wear reduces boost pressure and causes blue smoke.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Turbo Rebuild/Exchange - Replace or refurbish turbocharger unit only
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Turbo removal, exchange unit fitment or specialist rebuild, oil flush
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if engine otherwise healthy; 30-50k mile turbo expectancy

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt G8T Engine - Complete engine rebuild with balanced turbo included
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Bench-tested rebuilt engine, new turbo, EGR service, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage diesels; addresses turbo + underlying wear

problems.recommendation: If your Safrane diesel has exceeded 120k miles and shows turbo symptoms, investing in a full rebuilt G8T engine with a balanced turbo included often delivers better reliability and value than a standalone turbo replacement.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt G8T diesel engines for your Renault Safrane

problems.h4: Renault Safrane Timing Belt Failure - Catastrophic Interference Engine Risk
problems.affectedModels: All Renault Safrane petrol variants (1993-2001, N7Q/N7U/Z7X codes)
problems.rootCause: Rubber timing belt degradation over time; if it snaps, pistons collide with open valves causing catastrophic internal damage.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Belt Replacement - Replace belt, tensioner, water pump before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Belt kit fitment, tensioner, water pump, coolant flush
repairOptions.longevity: Essential preventative maintenance; recommended every 60k miles or 5 years

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Complete engine rebuild after belt failure damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Bench-tested rebuilt engine, new belt kit, upgraded components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option after catastrophic valve/piston damage; restores reliability

problems.recommendation: Never delay timing belt replacement on a Safrane petrol engine - the cost of prevention is a fraction of the repair after failure. If damage has occurred, a rebuilt engine with a new belt kit included is the most reliable solution.
problems.cta: Compare Renault Safrane petrol engine replacement prices now

problems.group: Renault Safrane EGR Valve Failure Diesel
problems.h4: Renault Safrane EGR Valve Failure Diesel - Limp Mode & Power Loss
problems.affectedModels: Renault Safrane 2.2 dT variants (1996-2001, G8T codes)
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup clogs EGR valve passages, restricting exhaust gas recirculation and triggering engine management limp mode.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - EGR Cleaning/Replacement - Remove, clean or replace EGR valve and associated pipework
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: EGR removal, ultrasonic cleaning or new valve fitment, system reset
repairOptions.longevity: Effective short-term fix; may recur if engine has high carbon buildup

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - EGR Blank + Software Remap - Physically blank EGR, adjust ECU mapping to prevent fault codes
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: EGR blanking plate fitment, ECU remap to disable EGR monitoring, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Popular with enthusiasts; eliminates recurrence but check MoT emissions compliance

problems.recommendation: For a classic Safrane used occasionally, EGR blanking with a professional remap offers a permanent solution to limp mode issues. For daily drivers, start with cleaning/replacement and monitor recurrence.
problems.cta: Find Renault Safrane diesel engine specialists for EGR solutions

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Renault Safrane
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing head gasket concerns on a V6 RXE, turbo wear on a high-mileage diesel, or timing belt anxiety on a petrol model, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Renault Safrane specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare quotes today and get your classic executive saloon back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Renault Safrane Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Renault Safrane and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Renault Safrane Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Renault Safrane in the UK is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement.

items.title: Renault Safrane 2.1L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.1L S8U diesel powered early Safrane RN and RT models from launch until 1996, offering modest torque for executive cruising but later superseded by the more refined 2.2L unit. Known for robust construction but limited power output compared to later diesels.
commonFailurePoints: injector pump degradation on high-mileage units
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Safrane 2.1L diesel engines

items.title: Renault Safrane 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.2L G8T diesel replaced the 2.1L from 1996, becoming the mainstream diesel choice across RN, RT, RXE and Initiale trims with improved torque and refinement. The most common diesel engine in UK Safrane replacement enquiries today.
commonFailurePoints: injector calibration drift on high-mileage examples
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Safrane 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Renault Safrane 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level 2.0L N7Q petrol engine powered base RN and RT Safrane models throughout production, offering adequate performance with simple SOHC design. Timing belt service critical due to interference engine design.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure causing catastrophic valve damage
water pump leaks on neglected units
items.cta: Compare Renault Safrane 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Renault Safrane 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.5L N7U petrol provided mid-range performance for RXE and early Initiale models, featuring a 12-valve design with stronger bottom end than the 2.0L. Popular with enthusiasts seeking smoother power delivery.
commonFailurePoints: valve stem seal wear leading to oil consumption on high-mileage units
items.cta: Compare Renault Safrane 2.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Renault Safrane 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The flagship 3.0L Z7X V6 petrol defined the Safrane RXE and Initiale, offering smooth, refined power befitting an executive saloon. The most sought-after engine by classic enthusiasts but prone to head gasket failure on high mileage.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure causing coolant/oil mixing
timing belt neglect leading to catastrophic interference damage
items.cta: Compare Renault Safrane 3.0L petrol engine prices

fuelTypes.h2: Renault Safrane Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Renault Safrane engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from the economical 2.1L diesel to the flagship 3.0L V6 petrol.

items.title: Renault Safrane Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel power defined the Safrane's executive appeal, with the 2.2L G8T becoming the most common replacement enquiry due to turbo and EGR wear on high-mileage examples.
items.descriptor: Diesel power defined the Safrane's executive appeal, with the 2.2L G8T becoming the most common replacement enquiry due to turbo and EGR wear on high-mileage examples.
items.familiesLabel: Common Renault Safrane Diesel Engine Families
families: G8T 700 - mainstream 2.2L diesel for RN/RT/RXE, EGR and turbo wear common after 100k miles
G8T 706 - late-production 2.2L for Initiale, revised ECU mapping but similar failure patterns
S8U 700 - early 2.1L diesel for RN/RT, robust but limited power, injector pump wear on neglected units
foundIn: B54 Pre-facelift (1993-1996) - S8U 700, early G8T variants
B54 Facelift (1996-2001) - G8T 700/706/710 across all diesel trims
knownFor: Strong motorway torque and fuel economy for executive cruising
Turbocharger oil seal degradation causing blue smoke and boost loss on high-mileage units
EGR valve clogging triggering limp mode, especially on short-journey cars
High UK replacement demand on RXE and Initiale diesel variants
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Safrane Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Renault Safrane Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Safranes offered smooth refinement from the entry 2.0L to the iconic 3.0L V6, with timing belt service critical across all variants due to interference engine design.
items.descriptor: Petrol Safranes offered smooth refinement from the entry 2.0L to the iconic 3.0L V6, with timing belt service critical across all variants due to interference engine design.
items.familiesLabel: Common Renault Safrane Petrol Engine Families
families: N7Q 700 - entry 2.0L SOHC for RN/RT, reliable if belt serviced, water pump leaks common
N7U 700 - mid-range 2.5L 12-valve for RXE, ignition coil wear and oil consumption on high mileage
Z7X 700 - flagship 3.0L V6 for RXE/Initiale, head gasket weakness the dominant failure pattern
foundIn: B54 All Years (1993-2001) - N7Q/N7U/Z7X across petrol trims, V6 exclusive to RXE/Initiale
knownFor: Smooth, refined power delivery befitting an executive saloon, especially the V6
Timing belt neglect causing catastrophic valve/piston collision on all petrol codes
Z7X V6 head gasket failure leading to coolant/oil mixing and white smoke symptoms
Strong enthusiast demand for rebuilt Z7X units for classic RXE/Initiale restorations
items.cta: Compare Renault Safrane Petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Renault Safrane Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline covers all major engine changes across the Safrane B54 production run (1993-2001), organised so classic car owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: B54 Launch: S8U Diesel & N7Q/N7U Petrol Debut
years.description: The Safrane launched with three core engines: the 2.1L S8U diesel for RN/RT, and 2.0L N7Q / 2.5L N7U petrol across the range.
keyChanges: Renault Safrane B54 platform launched in UK with RN, RT and RXE trims
S8U 700 2.1L diesel introduced for entry diesel variants
N7Q 700 2.0L and N7U 700 2.5L petrol engines available across petrol trims
Z7X 700 3.0L V6 flagship engine exclusive to RXE trim
knownFor: Launch-year build quality generally strong on low-mileage surviving examples
Early S8U diesel robust but underpowered compared to later 2.2L units
Z7X V6 head gasket weakness begins to emerge on high-mileage RXE models
Timing belt service critical on all petrol engines due to interference design
years.cta: Get quotes for 1993-1995 Renault Safrane replacement engines
years.ticker: Renault Safrane RXE V6 engine replacement • Safrane timing belt failure fix • Safrane diesel turbo replacement cost
enquiries: Renault Safrane RXE V6 engine replacement

years.preview: B54 Facelift: G8T Diesel & Z7X 701 V6 Introduced
years.description: The 1996 facelift brought the refined 2.2L G8T diesel across all trims and the updated Z7X 701 V6 for Initiale, defining the Safrane's mature specification.
keyChanges: G8T 700 2.2L diesel replaces S8U across RN/RT/RXE, offering improved torque and refinement
Initiale flagship trim launched with exclusive Z7X 701 V6 and luxury equipment
N7Q 704 and N7U 702 engine codes introduced for updated petrol variants
Emissions compliance updated to Euro 2 standards for 1997 model year
knownFor: G8T diesel becomes the most common Safrane engine in UK replacement enquiries
Z7X 701 V6 head gasket failure pattern becomes well-documented on high-mileage examples
Facelift models command premium among classic enthusiasts for improved refinement
EGR valve clogging emerges as common limp-mode trigger on diesel variants
years.cta: Compare Renault Safrane 1996-1997 engine replacement prices
years.ticker: Safrane G8T turbo replacement • Initiale V6 head gasket fix • Safrane EGR valve clogged solution

years.preview: B54 Final Production: G8T Refinements & Z7X Legacy
years.description: Final production years saw minor G8T diesel refinements and the Z7X V6 cementing its status as the iconic Safrane engine among classic enthusiasts.
keyChanges: G8T 710 code introduced with revised cooling system for improved reliability
Final UK sales of Safrane in 2001; production continued for export markets briefly
Classic vehicle registration eligibility begins for earliest 1993 examples
Aftermarket support for Z7X head gasket upgrades becomes widely available
knownFor: Late-production G8T units benefit from cooling system improvements reducing overheating risk
Z7X V6 remains the most sought-after engine for classic Safrane restorations
MoT exemption eligibility begins for 1993-1996 examples, boosting classic demand
Specialist rebuilders develop upgraded head gasket solutions for Z7X longevity
years.cta: Enquire about final-year Renault Safrane engine availability
years.ticker: Safrane classic vehicle engine replacement • Z7X upgraded head gasket kit • Safrane MoT exempt engine supply

faq.h2: Renault Safrane Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Renault Safrane engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt engine prices range from £895 for a used unit to £4,200 for a flagship Z7X V6 rebuild, with most common G8T diesel replacements falling between £1,450-£2,400 supply only.
keyPoints: Used engines start around £895 but carry higher risk and shorter warranty terms
Rebuilt G8T diesel engines typically cost £1,450-£2,400 supply only
Z7X V6 petrol rebuilds command £1,700-£4,200 due to complexity and enthusiast demand
Supply & fit labour adds £650-£1,200 depending on garage location and vehicle condition
items.cta: Compare Renault Safrane engine replacement prices from vetted UK specialists

items.question: What is the most reliable engine for the Renault Safrane?
items.answer: The G8T 2.2L diesel offers the best balance of reliability, parts availability and running costs for classic Safrane ownership, provided turbo and EGR maintenance is addressed.
keyPoints: G8T diesel robust block design tolerates high mileage with proper servicing
Z7X V6 offers refinement but head gasket weakness requires preventative attention
N7Q 2.0L petrol simplest mechanically but timing belt neglect causes catastrophic failure
Regular oil changes and cooling system maintenance critical for all Safrane engines
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt G8T diesel engines for your Renault Safrane

items.question: Can I fit a different engine code in my Renault Safrane?
items.answer: Cross-code engine swaps are possible but require ECU recalibration, wiring harness adaptation and DVLA notification; we recommend matching your original code for simplest compliance.
keyPoints: Z7X 700 to Z7X 701 swaps generally straightforward within V6 family
Diesel to petrol or vice versa requires extensive electronics and mounting modifications
DVLA must be notified of any engine code change for registration accuracy
Specialist garages experienced with classic Renault electronics essential for adaptation work
items.cta: Enquire about Renault Safrane engine compatibility verification before ordering

items.question: How long does a Renault Safrane engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines typically dispatch within 1-3 working days; full supply & fit services require 2-4 days garage time depending on specialist availability and vehicle condition.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines bench-tested before dispatch to minimise post-fit issues
Classic Safrane chassis may require additional rust remediation during engine removal
ECU coding and road test included in professional supply & fit services
Urgent next-day dispatch available for in-stock engine codes via select specialists
items.cta: Find Renault Safrane engine supply & fit specialists with next-day availability

items.question: What warranty comes with a reconditioned Renault Safrane engine?
items.answer: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt/reconditioned units via EngineMarket, with used engines typically carrying 3-month coverage.
keyPoints: Warranty covers manufacturing defects and premature component failure
Labour costs for warranty claims vary by supplier; confirm terms before ordering
Upgraded components like multi-layer steel head gaskets often extend practical longevity beyond warranty period
Keep service records and use recommended fluids to maintain warranty validity
items.cta: Compare Renault Safrane engine warranty terms from UK specialists before committing

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a Renault Safrane?
items.answer: For classic RXE/Initiale models in good condition, a rebuilt engine often represents better value than vehicle replacement, preserving heritage and avoiding depreciation on a newer car.
keyPoints: Low-mileage Safrane Initiale values rising among classic executive saloon enthusiasts
Rebuilt engine cost typically 30-50% of equivalent classic car purchase price
MoT exemption for pre-1996 examples reduces ongoing compliance costs
Specialist rebuilds with upgraded components can exceed original reliability
items.cta: Use our repair vs replace calculator to assess your Renault Safrane's value proposition

items.question: How do I find my Renault Safrane engine code?
items.answer: Check the engine identification plate on the cylinder head or timing cover, or enter your registration on EngineMarket for instant DVLA-verified code lookup.
keyPoints: Engine code format: letter-number combination e.g., Z7X 700, G8T 706
Plate typically located on cylinder head, timing cover or near radiator support
VIN decoder tools can cross-reference chassis number to original engine code
Photograph the plate when ordering to ensure exact compatibility verification
items.cta: Enter your registration for instant Renault Safrane engine code verification

items.question: What causes Renault Safrane engine failure most commonly?
items.answer: Z7X V6 head gasket failure and G8T diesel turbo wear account for the majority of catastrophic Safrane engine replacements in the UK classic car market.
keyPoints: Z7X head gaskets fail due to thermal cycling and original material limitations
G8T turbo oil seals degrade after 100k miles, causing boost loss and blue smoke
Timing belt neglect on petrol engines causes catastrophic interference damage
EGR clogging on diesels triggers limp mode but rarely causes terminal failure
items.cta: Compare solutions for your specific Renault Safrane engine failure symptom

items.question: Can I get finance for a Renault Safrane engine replacement?
items.answer: Specialist classic car finance options exist for engine replacements via select lenders, typically requiring vehicle valuation and proof of restoration intent.
keyPoints: Finance available for vehicles valued over £3,000 with clear ownership history
Interest rates vary by lender; compare APR before committing to repayment plan
Some specialist garages offer in-house payment plans for supply & fit packages
Classic car insurance may require notification of major mechanical work like engine replacement
items.cta: Enquire about Renault Safrane engine replacement finance eligibility with our partner lenders

items.question: Do you supply engines for imported Renault Safrane models?
items.answer: Yes, we supply engines for European-spec LHD Safrane imports, with compatibility verification and adaptation support available via our specialist network.
keyPoints: Engine codes generally compatible between UK RHD and European LHD Safrane models
ECU calibration may require adjustment for different emissions specifications
Mounting points and ancillary fittings typically identical across markets
DVLA notification required for any engine code change on imported vehicles
items.cta: Get quotes for imported Renault Safrane engine compatibility verification and supply
